title: ericameria parryi subsp. howardii
intro: Ericameria parryi subsp. howardii, also known as Howard's greasewood or Bigberry greasewood, is a plant species in the sunflower family native to the southwestern United States. This shrub has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial abilities, which can help soothe wounds, reduce pain, and combat infections. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in essential oils and contain compounds like sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have health benefits such as antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. In herbal preparations, Ericameria parryi subsp. howardii is often used to make teas, salves, and infusions that can be applied topically to the skin or ingested to promote overall health and well-being.
